note: Independent BLIND vision reads of the 5 concentrated-risk Baritone (OLIVER) page-read rows. G2: vision = corroboration, never court. Each crop = one bass-clef staff, 6 flats (Db/Bb), 4x upscaled, from the FROZEN page jpgs. Claude reads here; Argus (gemini) reads folded in separately. Court of record = Jon (A4).
key: 6 flats — B E A D G C flat by default (Db major / Bb minor)

claude.obs: whole note sits HIGH on the staff (~4th line F3 / top region); tentatively consistent with F3

Baritone:5.two_eye: SCATTER — claude~F3 vs argus Ab3; eyes agree the note is HIGH but differ on exact line. Uncertain.
Baritone:5.flag: m5(high) vs m9(low) BOTH claimed F3 — cross-check the pair; restored-from-silence bar

claude.obs: whole note sits LOW on the staff, appears LOWER than the claimed F3 (4th line)

argus.obs: whole note on the first ledger line BELOW the bass staff; no accidental

Baritone:9.two_eye: STRONG AGREEMENT (direction) — BOTH eyes read this well BELOW the claimed F3 (4th line). The engraving F3 is a genuine pitch suspect.
Baritone:9.flag: TOP PRIORITY — two independent eyes both read LOW (below staff); engraving F3 likely too high. Page-image-read per corrections file.

claude.obs: upper-staff note PRECEDED by an editorial '(b)' parenthetical flat — page's own marking confirms a flatted C => Cb4

argus.obs: whole note on the top line (A3) with a flat-in-parentheses drawn before it

Baritone:21.two_eye: PARTIAL — BOTH eyes SEE the editorial parenthetical flat (=> a flatted note), but disagree on line (claude Cb4 vs argus Ab3). Flat confirmed; exact pitch uncertain.
Baritone:21.flag: page editorial flat present (supports a flatted note); confirm Cb4 vs Ab3

claude.obs: restored-from-silence bar; cannot cleanly map 'hop' syllable to a single notehead across the context crop

argus.obs: HIGH: the notes on 'hop-ing' are QUARTER notes (solid noteheads), NOT whole/half; the only whole notes in view fall on 'same' two bars later

Baritone:25.two_eye: PITCH suspect (from-silence reconstruction); rhythm DOWNGRADED. Argus flagged quarters, but the homophonic Lead m25 is ALSO two half-notes (Lead E4:half + Eb4:half — and Lead's rhythm came from Audiveris's real page-read, only its pitch was corrected). So the half-note rhythm is structurally corroborated; Argus likely mis-segmented (it placed the whole-notes on 'same', the wrong bar).
Baritone:25.structural: Lead m25 = E4:half + Eb4:half (homophonic) corroborates Baritone 2-half rhythm. Pitch Db4 'hop' still a from-silence reconstruction.
Baritone:25.flag: PITCH still needs Jon (Db4 'hop' reconstructed); rhythm probably fine (Lead corroborates halves). 'ing'=Bb3 (row 201) is the HIGH Eb-triad completion.

claude.obs: fermata note in the MIDDLE of the staff (~middle line D3 / 3rd space E3)

argus.obs: whole note on the first ledger line ABOVE the staff (C4); no accidental; fermata present

Baritone:29.two_eye: SCATTER (wide) — claude mid-staff (~Eb3/D3) vs argus above-staff (Cb4). Strong disagreement; genuinely uncertain.
Baritone:29.flag: cadential fermata; eyes disagree widely — needs Jon to set Eb3 vs alternatives
